channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden health officials say they built up US pandemic defenses. Trump promises changes
item.description: The Biden administration is releasing a “roadmap” for maintaining government defenses against infectious diseases, just as President-elect Donald Trump pledges to dismantle some of them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prosecutor who investigated Hunter Biden defends probes, denounces president's remarks in new report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The criminal charges against Hunter Biden “were the culmination of thorough, impartial investigations, not partisan politics,” the prosecutor who led the probes said in a report that sharply criticized President Joe Biden for having maligned the Justice Department when he pardoned
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Utah's push to wrest control of public land from the federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court turned back a push by the state of Utah to wrest control of vast areas of public land from the federal government, marking a small victory for land conservation advocates who worry that similar efforts may escalate in a Republican-controlled Washington.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Live: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: The Freedom Caucus takes over the Wyoming House, marking its first chance to lead
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump plans bold moves for his first days in office, so too are conservative lawmakers in Wyoming, the first state where Trump-friendly Freedom Caucus members have won control of a statehouse chamber.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An American tradition: Defeated candidates attending the president-elect's inauguration
item.description: In January 1981, Jimmy Carter nodded politely toward Ronald Reagan as the new Republican president thanked the Democrat for his administration's help after Reagan resoundingly defeated Carter the previous November.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to watch as Pete Hegseth faces questions about his experience and personal past
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth will publicly face senators for the first time Tuesday after weeks of privately pushing back on criticism over his qualifications and personal past.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth vows a 'warrior culture' if confirmed as Trump's defense secretary, confronting critics
item.description: Pete Hegseth vows to bring “warrior culture’” to the military if confirmed as President-elect Donald Trump’s defense secretary pick.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley bill past second hurdle
item.description: The Laken Riley Act on Monday cleared a second procedural hurdle in the Senate as talk turns to an amendment push by Democrats in order to potentially make alterations to the bill. Senators voted 82-10 on the motion to proceed to the legislation, allowing the chamber to begin debate.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. Liz Warren lays out more than 100 questions she wants Pete Hegseth to answer during confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren, the top Democrat on the Senate Armed Services subcommittee on personnel, presented more than 70 questions in a 33-page letter to Trump's nominee for Defense Secretary ahead of his confirmation hearing this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis calls special session to implement Trump's immigration policies
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is calling state lawmakers into a special session to help carry out President-elect Donald Trump's promises for a swift crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First hearing in 'Trump-proof' California special session canceled as chairman's district hit by wildfires
item.description: A California Assembly committee chairman canceled the planned first hearing in a special "Trump-proof" session due to wildfires ravaging his legislative district.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump team is questioning civil servants at National Security Council about commitment to his agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Incoming senior Trump administration officials have begun questioning career civil servants who work on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for in the 2024 election, their political contributions and whether they have made social media posts that could be
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces student debt relief for 150,000 borrowers, bringing total helped to more than 5M
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it was forgiving student loans for more than 150,000 borrowers, bringing the total number of individuals impacted by debt relief in President Biden’s term to over 5 million.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate District 60 special election
item.description: Minnesota Senate District 60 is holding a special election to fill the vacancy left by DFL Sen. Kari Dziedzic who died last month.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Incoming Trump team questions civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: Incoming senior Trump officials have begun questioning career civil servants serving on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for, their political contributions in the 2024 election and whether they've made social media posts that could be considered incriminating by the Tru
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How to watch Trump's inauguration on NewsNation
item.description: Donald Trump will be inaugurated as the 47th U.S. president on Jan. 20 for his second nonconsecutive term.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Jack Smith shouldn't be allowed to release report
item.description: "Why would Deranged Jack Smith be allowed to issue a “report” on a complete and total Witch Hunt against me, strictly for political purposes,” Trump said in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans urge Trump nominees: Don't take the bait from Democrats
item.description: Senate Republicans are urging President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees to resist taking the bait from Democrats when their high-stakes confirmation hearings kick off next week, marking a make-or-break moment for some of his choices.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards Pope Francis the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction
item.description: The White House commended Pope Francis for his commitment to serving the poor, as well as advocating for peace and protecting the planet.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith resigns after submitting Trump report
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned from the Justice Department after submitting his investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pence said he congratulated Trump on his win at Carter’s funeral
item.description: The Pences were seated a row behind Trump with the other vice presidents, while Trump sat next to former President Obama.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: California's working class overlooked amid wildfires, US rep says
item.description: Rep. Maxine Waters, D-Calif., called for attention to the working class people in Altadena, California, who have “limited resources" amid the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Hochul defends congestion pricing, explains inflation rebate checks
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In a few days, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will deliver her State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Homeless mental health and affordability headline NYC mayor’s State of the City address
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday laid out an ambitious agenda for 2025 while fighting for his political life. His State of the City address was the last of his first term in office as he faces a tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5
